_comment: Release Engineering scorecard TEMPLATE (Req 11) — tracked in git, unlike release-inputs.json (gitignored generated artifact). Holds the rubric-based dimensions (reliability, performance) and the health/thresholds a maintainer must set by judgment, since they cannot be measured live. scripts/release/derive-inputs.mjs merges this template with LIVE security+coverage evidence on every CI run to produce the actual release-inputs.json the enforcement gate reads. Update this file, not release-inputs.json, to change thresholds or rubric scores.

reliability.source: Maintainer assessment grounded in consistently green reliability gates: memory-safety, chaos-testing, fuzz-testing, load-testing, and infrastructure system suites (Node 20 + 22), MySQL/PostgreSQL E2E certification, and property-based test suites.
reliability.note: Rubric-based score, not a single external measurement. Adjust to reflect formal reliability SLAs as they are defined.

performance.source: Maintainer assessment grounded in the benchmark regression gate (packages/core benchmarks vs committed baseline.json) passing with no >10% throughput regression.
performance.note: Rubric-based score. Raise once a normalized performance index is computed from benchmark output.

_evidence.thresholds_rationale: coverage/security thresholds are set to a bar the project has historically met; they are honest current-state bars, not an aspirational default. security and coverage themselves are now derived LIVE each run by derive-inputs.mjs (OpenSSF Scorecard API; packages/core coverage/lcov.info) — review these thresholds periodically against the live-measured values and raise them as quality improves.
